meta.hypothesis: Applying RECOMB_BOOST only to sub-cutoff and autoionization branches (leaving tracked-secondary recomb at un-boosted Onsager) preserves the chemistry G-value improvement from joint-fix v1 while recovering the cascade-ion regression. Rationale: tracked-secondary eaq thermalizes 5-10 nm from H2O+, so time-integrated recomb adds little opportunity there.
meta.passBar: Asymmetric variant must (a) keep RMS dev vs chem6 at 0.1 ps below 22% (close to v1's 19%) AND (b) recover cascade ions to within 5% of pre-fix (i.e., > 354). PASS iff both met.

sources.shaders_used: src/shaders/primary.wgsl line 310 and src/shaders/secondary.wgsl line 213 had RECOMB_BOOST removed (one-shot Onsager only on tracked-secondary). After capture, both reverted to uniform RECOMB_BOOST. Committed shaders are the v1 (uniform) version.
sources.live_e5d_v2: Captured from a live run; the artifact file was overwritten by the subsequent v1 re-run.

diagnosis: Asymmetric variant fails pass bar: chemistry_ok=false, cascade_ok=true. Removing RECOMB_BOOST from tracked-secondary recovers cascade ions (381.1 > 354) but reverts chemistry close to baseline (RMS dev 27.9% vs v1's 19%). **The tracked-secondary recomb branch is the dominant lever for BOTH effects — they cannot be decoupled by selective application of this knob.** Genuine third knob needs to be physics-driven (H2O+ tracking with proper time-integration, where recomb fires only on actual encounters).

summary.headline: v2 cascade=381.1 (vs v1 344.6, pre-fix 371.9); v2 RMS_dev=27.9% (vs v1 19%, pre-fix 30.3%). v2 trades chemistry for cascade — structural tradeoff is real.

rows.note: v2 reverts chemistry close to baseline. The asymmetric application did NOT preserve the v1 chemistry benefit.
v2 recovers cascade ions above pre-fix — this part of the hypothesis IS supported.
v2 also recovers W-value below pre-fix; further from ICRU 31 target than v1 only because v1 was further to begin with.
v1 G(H2) 0.197 vs v2 0.150 — v2 loses most of the chemistry improvement. Confirms tracked-secondary path is the dominant chemistry lever.
CSDA preserved under v2 — σ_exc effect dominates here, not the recomb branch.
